description_long: This is a military-style twin-boom airplane with a central fuselage and two engines mounted on the wings. The aircraft is painted in olive green with black and white invasion stripes on the wings and tail booms, and features red tips on the vertical stabilizers. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, connecting the central fuselage to the twin tail booms, which terminate in vertical stabilizers joined by a horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is located at the front of the central fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a clear canopy. The airplane displays U.S. Air Force insignia on the wings and fuselage, and the propellers are positioned at the front of each engine nacelle. The overall design is reminiscent of World War II-era fighter or reconnaissance aircraft, optimized for speed and maneuverability.
description: A twin-boom, olive green military airplane with black and white invasion stripes, red tail tips, and dual front-mounted propeller engines. Features an enclosed forward cockpit and U.S. Air Force insignia.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin propellers, central cockpit, and the straight wings with invasion stripes.
description_view_1: Top-down angled view highlighting the twin-boom structure, red-tipped vertical stabilizers, and U.S. insignia on the wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin tail booms, horizontal stabilizer, and the back of the engine nacelles.
description_view_3: Top-down angled view from the opposite side, again showing the twin-boom layout and invasion stripes.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets, featuring black and white invasion stripes and a twin-boom connection.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a front-facing propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit in the central fuselage, likely single or tandem seat with a clear canopy.
